    <div><div class="footerContainer"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;" class="ctHeaderFooterPage">27</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span></p></div><hr class="pageBreak" style="margin:18pt 0pt; ct-page-break: true; page-break-after: always;" /><div class="headerContainer"></div></div>
    <div><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span id="_Aci_Pg28" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Mobivity Holdings Corp.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:NatureOfOperations" id="ct-nonNumeric-1a255a98-e654-4545-a2e6-194bd6318a59" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-1a255a98-e654-4545-a2e6-194bd6318a59-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">1. Nature of Operations</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-1a255a98-e654-4545-a2e6-194bd6318a59-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Mobivity Holdings Corp. (the &#8220;Company&#8221; or &#8220;we&#8221;) is in the business of developing and operating proprietary platforms over which brands and enterprises can conduct national and localized, data-driven marketing campaigns.&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Mobivity&#8217;s Recurrency platform enables multi-unit retailers to leverage the power of their own data to yield maximum customer spend, frequency and loyalty while achieving the highest Return on Marketing Spend (ROMS) possible. Mobivity&#8217;s customers use Recurrency to:</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0.50in;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-indent: -0.25in;vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;display: inline-block;font-family: Symbol;font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;text-indent: 0;width: 0.250in;">&#61623;</span><span id="_Aci_An10_3F" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span id="_Aci_Pa10_2D31387C3336" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Transform messy point-of-sale (POS) data collected from thousands of points of sale into usable intelligence.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0.50in;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-indent: -0.25in;vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;display: inline-block;font-family: Symbol;font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;text-indent: 0;width: 0.250in;">&#61623;</span><span id="_Aci_An11_3F" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span id="_Aci_Pa11_2D31387C3336" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Measure, predict, and boost guest frequency and spend by channel.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0.50in;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-indent: -0.25in;vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;display: inline-block;font-family: Symbol;font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;text-indent: 0;width: 0.250in;">&#61623;</span><span id="_Aci_An12_3F" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span id="_Aci_Pa12_2D31387C3336" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Deploy and manage one-time use offer codes and attribute sales accurately across every channel, promotion and media program.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0.50in;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-indent: -0.25in;vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;display: inline-block;font-family: Symbol;font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;text-indent: 0;width: 0.250in;">&#61623;</span><span id="_Aci_An13_3F" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span id="_Aci_Pa13_2D31387C3336" style="text-decoration: none;"></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Deliver 1:1 promotions and offers with customized Mobile Messaging, Personalized Receipt Promotions and Integrated Loyalty programs.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Mobivity&#8217;s Recurrency, delivered as a SaaS platform, is used by leading brands including Subway, Sonic Drive-In, Chick-fil-A, Checkers/Rally&#8217;s and Circle K&#8217;s across more than </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-f890400b-f9d3-4439-a3b1-1881d194bcf7" name="mfon:NumberOfRetailLocationsWhereSaasPlatformIsUsed" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021_srt_RangeAxis_srt_MinimumMember" unitRef="Unit14" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">40,000</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> retail locations globally.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We&#8217;re living in a data-driven economy. In fact, by 2003 &#8212; when the concept of &#8220;big data&#8221; became common vernacular in marketing - as much data was being created every two days as had been created in all of time prior to 2003. Today, Big Data has grown at such a rate that 90% of the world&#8217;s data has been created in the past two years. Unfortunately, despite there being so much data accumulated, only one percent of data is being utilized today by most businesses.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The challenge for multi-unit retailers isn&#8217;t that they don&#8217;t have enough data; in fact, national retailers are collecting millions of detailed transactions daily from thousands of points of sale around the world. The challenge is being able to make sense of this transaction data, which is riddled with data entry errors, collected by multiple POS systems and complicated by a taxonomy compiled by thousands of different franchisee owners. To normalize such an overwhelming amount of data into usable intelligence and then leverage it to optimize media investment and promotion strategy requires numerous teams of data analysts and data scientists that many retailers and restaurant operators simply don&#8217;t have. Which is why so many technology and data companies, that can help solve these challenges, have been invested in and acquired by brands including, McDonald&#8217;s, Starbucks and Yum Brands.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Mobivity&#8217;s Recurrency platform fills this need with a self-service SaaS offering, enabling operators to intelligently optimize their promotions, media and marketing spend. Recurrency drives system-wide sales producing on average a </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-ef92bdc8-e7b2-4a4e-ac15-968b51c11a26" name="mfon:AveragePercentIncreaseInGuestSpendingUsingCompanyPlatform" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit13" decimals="2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" scale="-2">13</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">% increase in guest spend and a </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-0ad37b8c-5643-49a3-9dce-dd509a411fa4" name="mfon:PercentOfImprovementInFrequencyUsingCompanyPlatform" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit13" decimals="2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" scale="-2">26</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">% improvement in frequency, ultimately delivering an average Return on Marketing Spend of 10X. In other words, for every dollar invested in marketing, retailers using Recurrency to manage, optimize and deliver multi-channel&#160;consumer promotions generate an average of ten dollars in </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: italic;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">incremental</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> revenue from their customers.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We generate revenue by charging the resellers, brands and enterprises a per-message transactional fee, or through fixed or variable software licensing fees.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:SignificantAccountingPoliciesTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">2. Summary of Significant Accounting Policies</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ConsolidationPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-e866ef29-4d2c-4576-bb1d-84933dab21c3" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-e866ef29-4d2c-4576-bb1d-84933dab21c3-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Principles of Consolidation</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-e866ef29-4d2c-4576-bb1d-84933dab21c3-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its wholly-owned subsidiary, Live Lenz Inc. All significant intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:UseOfEstimates" id="ct-nonNumeric-81d51080-63d2-47ab-bc4b-6686f36622bb" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-81d51080-63d2-47ab-bc4b-6686f36622bb-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The preparation of financial statements in conformity with GAAP requires management to make estimates and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities and disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the financial statements and the reported amounts of expenses during the reporting period. Significant estimates used are those related to stock-based compensation, asset impairments, the valuation and useful lives of depreciable tangible and certain intangible assets, the fair value of common stock used in acquisitions of businesses, the fair value of assets and liabilities acquired in acquisitions of businesses, the fair value of options issued with related party debt, and the valuation allowance of deferred tax assets. Management believes that these estimates are reasonable; however, actual results may differ from these estimates.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation></div>
    <div><div class="footerContainer"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;" class="ctHeaderFooterPage">28</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span></p></div><hr class="pageBreak" style="margin:18pt 0pt; ct-page-break: true; page-break-after: always;" /><div class="headerContainer"></div></div>
    <ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-1" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-2"><div><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:PriorPeriodReclassificationAdjustmentDescription" id="ct-nonNumeric-8e997f8d-9e64-459d-8466-232a6c3ff154" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8e997f8d-9e64-459d-8466-232a6c3ff154-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We account for acquired businesses using the purchase method of accounting. Under the purchase method, our consolidated financial statements reflect the operations of an acquired business starting from the completion of the acquisition. In addition, the assets acquired and liabilities assumed are recorded at the date of acquisition at their respective estimated fair values, with any excess of the purchase price over the estimated fair values of the net assets acquired recorded as goodwill.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; We grant unsecured credit to substantially all of our customers. Ongoing credit evaluations are performed, and potential credit losses are charged to operations at the time the account receivable is estimated to be uncollectible. Since we cannot necessarily predict future changes in the financial stability of our customers, we cannot guarantee that our reserves will continue to be adequate.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:GoodwillAndIntangibleAssetsPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-7a055bc2-8e55-4045-a759-a0344583dbbd" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-7a055bc2-8e55-4045-a759-a0344583dbbd-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Goodwill and Intangible Assets</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-7a055bc2-8e55-4045-a759-a0344583dbbd-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-7a055bc2-8e55-4045-a759-a0344583dbbd-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Goodwill is tested for impairment at a minimum on an annual basis. Goodwill is tested for impairment at the reporting unit level by first performing a qualitative assessment to determine whether it is more likely than not that the fair value of the reporting unit is less than its carrying value. If the reporting unit does not pass the qualitative assessment, then the reporting unit&apos;s carrying value is compared to its fair value. The fair values of the reporting units are estimated using market and discounted cash flow approaches. Goodwill is considered impaired if the carrying value of the reporting unit exceeds its fair value. The discounted cash flow approach uses expected future operating results. Failure to achieve these expected results may cause a future impairment of goodwill at the reporting unit. </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We conducted our annual impairment tests of goodwill as of December&#160;31, 2021 and 2020. As a result of these tests, we a total impairment charges of $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-87fefac1-cc55-4bfe-99e7-b417892067a2" name="us-gaap:GoodwillImpairmentLoss" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="INF" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">85,169</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Intangible assets consist of patents and trademarks, purchased customer contracts, purchased customer and merchant relationships, purchased trade names, purchased technology, and non-compete agreements. Intangible assets are amortized over the period of estimated benefit using the straight-line method and estimated useful lives ranging from </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; -sec-ix-hidden:ct-nonNumeric-451199df-f6dc-4e66-8af9-e78daa994ec3;">one</span></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> to </span><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:FiniteLivedIntangibleAssetUsefulLife" id="ct-nonNumeric-25547222-e9c9-4fdb-8af6-5dca5ccf7d52" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021_srt_RangeAxis_srt_MaximumMember" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">twenty years</span></ix:nonNumeric><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">. No significant residual value is estimated for intangible assets. We evaluate long-lived assets (including intangible assets) for impairment whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate that the carrying amount of a long-lived asset may not be recoverable. An asset is considered impaired if its carrying amount exceeds the undiscounted future net cash flow the asset is expected to generate.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-7a055bc2-8e55-4045-a759-a0344583dbbd-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The Company&#8217;s evaluation of its long-lived assets completed resulted in $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-b702d2c0-25ac-49f1-bb98-cf95a355132b" name="us-gaap:TangibleAssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">8286</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> and $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-780b3b57-5f36-415a-852a-7dce1ceaed1a" name="us-gaap:TangibleAssetImpairmentCharges" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">0</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> of intangible impairment expense during the years ended December 31, 2021 and December 31, 2020.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></div></ix:continuation>
    <div><div class="footerContainer"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;" class="ctHeaderFooterPage">29</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span></p></div><hr class="pageBreak" style="margin:18pt 0pt; ct-page-break: true; page-break-after: always;" /><div class="headerContainer"></div></div>
    <ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-2" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-3"><div><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:InternalUseSoftwarePolicy" id="ct-nonNumeric-12ca5ade-a4e0-45fe-bd91-48417f03b990" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-12ca5ade-a4e0-45fe-bd91-48417f03b990-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Software Development Costs</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-12ca5ade-a4e0-45fe-bd91-48417f03b990-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-12ca5ade-a4e0-45fe-bd91-48417f03b990-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Software development costs include direct costs incurred for internally developed products and payments made to independent software developers and/or contract engineers. The Company accounts for software development costs in accordance with the FASB guidance for the costs of computer software to be sold, leased, or otherwise marketed (&#8220;ASC Subtopic 985-20&#8221;). Software development costs are capitalized once the technological feasibility of a product is established and such costs are determined to be recoverable. Technological feasibility of a product encompasses technical design documentation and integration documentation, or the completed and tested product design and working model. Technological feasibility is evaluated on a project-by-project basis. Amounts related to software development that are not capitalized are charged immediately to the appropriate expense account. Amounts that are considered &#8216;research and development&#8217; that are not capitalized are immediately charged to engineering, research, and development expense.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Capitalized costs for those products that are cancelled or abandoned are charged to impairment expense in the period of cancellation. Commencing upon product release, capitalized software development costs are amortized to &#8220;Amortization Expense - Development&#8221; based on the straight-line method over a </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; -sec-ix-hidden:ct-nonNumeric-bd09aaae-095f-43ae-8a58-00629967f94f;">twenty-four month</span></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> period.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">&#160;</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-12ca5ade-a4e0-45fe-bd91-48417f03b990-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The Company evaluates the future recoverability of capitalized software development costs on an annual basis. For products that have been released in prior years, the primary evaluation criterion is ongoing relations with the customer. The Company&#8217;s evaluation of its capitalized software development asset resulted in impairment charges of $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-9f44e70b-bd59-4a4f-8552-9f5b91c8d85c" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfIntangibleAssetsFinitelived" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021_us-gaap_FiniteLivedIntangibleAssetsByMajorClassAxis_us-gaap_ComputerSoftwareIntangibleAssetMember" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">0</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> for the year ended December&#160;31, 2021 and $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-182f47e5-3319-4871-a4ab-11cdd1894a9e" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfIntangibleAssetsFinitelived" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020_us-gaap_FiniteLivedIntangibleAssetsByMajorClassAxis_us-gaap_ComputerSoftwareIntangibleAssetMember" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="INF" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">8,886</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> for the year ended December&#160;31, 2020.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="background: #FFFFFF;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOrDisposalOfLongLivedAssetsPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-b8bae4b5-e6f7-4145-b7ec-387027a63161" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-b8bae4b5-e6f7-4145-b7ec-387027a63161-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Impairment of Long-Lived Assets</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-b8bae4b5-e6f7-4145-b7ec-387027a63161-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We evaluate long-lived assets (including intangible assets) for impairment whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate that the carrying amount of a long-lived asset may not be recoverable. An asset is considered impaired if its carrying amount exceeds the undiscounted future net cash flow the asset is expected to generate.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ForeignCurrencyTransactionsAndTranslationsPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-f4499e98-6eec-4960-9c4d-d014d6c99671" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-f4499e98-6eec-4960-9c4d-d014d6c99671-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Foreign Currency Translation</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-f4499e98-6eec-4960-9c4d-d014d6c99671-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">The Company translates the financial statements of its foreign subsidiary from the local (functional) currency into US Dollars using the year or reporting period end or average exchange rates in accordance with the requirements of Accounting Standards Codification subtopic 830-10, Foreign Currency Matters (&#8220;ASC 830-10&#8221;). Assets and liabilities of these subsidiaries were translated at exchange rates as of the balance sheet date. Revenues and expenses are translated at average rates in effect for the periods presented. The cumulative translation adjustment is included in the accumulated other comprehensive gain (loss) within shareholders&#8217; equity. Foreign currency transaction gains and losses arising from exchange rate fluctuations on transactions denominated in a currency other than the functional currency are included in the unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income and Comprehensive Income.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:DerivativesReportingOfDerivativeActivity" id="ct-nonNumeric-e21cbc6b-d2a6-4ec9-b068-5a054596b745" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-e21cbc6b-d2a6-4ec9-b068-5a054596b745-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Derivative Financial Instruments</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-e21cbc6b-d2a6-4ec9-b068-5a054596b745-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We do not use derivative instruments to hedge exposures to cash flow, market or foreign currency risks.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We review the terms of the common stock, warrants and convertible debt we issue to determine whether there are embedded derivative instruments, including embedded conversion options, which are required to be bifurcated and accounted for separately as derivative financial instruments. In circumstances where the host instrument contains more than one embedded derivative instrument, including the conversion option, that is required to be bifurcated, the bifurcated derivative instruments are accounted for as a single, compound derivative instrument.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:RevenueRecognitionPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-4e0db8f2-401c-44b2-99d9-f25390cefdd7" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-4e0db8f2-401c-44b2-99d9-f25390cefdd7-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Revenue Recognition and Concentrations </span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-4e0db8f2-401c-44b2-99d9-f25390cefdd7-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-4e0db8f2-401c-44b2-99d9-f25390cefdd7-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Our Recurrency platform is a hosted solution. We generate revenue from licensing our software to clients in our software as a service model, per-message and per-minute transactional fees, and customized professional services. We recognize license/subscription fees over the period of the contract, service fees as the services are performed, and per-message or per-minute transaction revenue when the transaction takes place. </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Under Topic 606, revenue is recognized when control of the promised goods or services is transferred to our customers, in an amount that reflects the consideration we expect to be entitled to in exchange for those goods or services. </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We consider authoritative guidance on multiple deliverables in determining whether each deliverable represents a separate unit of accounting. Some customers are billed on a month-to-month basis with no contractual term and are collected by credit card. Revenue is recognized at the time that the services are rendered, and the selling price is fixed with a set range of plans. Cash received in advance of the performance of services is recorded as deferred revenue.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-4e0db8f2-401c-44b2-99d9-f25390cefdd7-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">During the years ended December&#160;31, 2021 and 2020, </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-f5a2f317-ab3d-42bb-812b-8ff74f182ef6" name="mfon:NumberOfSignificantCustomers" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember" unitRef="Unit18" decimals="INF" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-d04b2aad-5019-474a-a76b-a2a7a93ebf19" name="mfon:NumberOfSignificantCustomers" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember" unitRef="Unit18" decimals="INF" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen">two</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> customers accounted for </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-a4a7064e-7842-4799-8a16-ad44f98f01e1" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021_srt_MajorCustomersAxis_mfon_TwoCustomersMember_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_us-gaap_CustomerConcentrationRiskMember" unitRef="Unit13" decimals="2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" scale="-2">55</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">% and </span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-8cf6d0eb-f0e7-4dde-9b1e-9eb68dc03ecc" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020_srt_MajorCustomersAxis_mfon_TwoCustomersMember_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember_us-gaap_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis_us-gaap_CustomerConcentrationRiskMember" unitRef="Unit13" decimals="2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" scale="-2">77</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">% of our revenues, respectively.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></div></ix:continuation>
    <div><div class="footerContainer"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: center;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;" class="ctHeaderFooterPage">30</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">-</span></p></div><hr class="pageBreak" style="margin:18pt 0pt; ct-page-break: true; page-break-after: always;" /><div class="headerContainer"></div></div>
    <ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-3" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8512d14c-3737-492f-828d-d850fa3b76be-continuation-4"><div><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ComprehensiveIncomePolicyPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-f17fa855-5669-472e-aae8-bd4be1c11b8f" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-f17fa855-5669-472e-aae8-bd4be1c11b8f-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Comprehensive Income (Loss) </span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-f17fa855-5669-472e-aae8-bd4be1c11b8f-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Comprehensive loss is defined as the change in equity during a period from transactions and other events and circumstances from non-owner sources. We are required to record all components of comprehensive loss in the consolidated financial statements in the period in which they are recognized. Net loss and other comprehensive loss, including foreign currency translation adjustments and unrealized gains and losses on investments, are reported, net of their related tax effect, to arrive at comprehensive loss. For the twelve months ended December 31, 2021 and 2020, the comprehensive loss was $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-77896106-b866-477f-aaca-0140a827cbd6" name="us-gaap:ComprehensiveIncomeNetOfTax" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" sign="-">8,288,426</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> and $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-3d2ec6f9-2798-4597-9407-b04050e56a72" name="us-gaap:ComprehensiveIncomeNetOfTax" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" sign="-">2,950,623</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; color: #000000;font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> respectively. </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationOptionAndIncentivePlansPolicy" id="ct-nonNumeric-3e2500ca-439a-4d89-a91e-8e5ae3bb990b" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-3e2500ca-439a-4d89-a91e-8e5ae3bb990b-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Stock-based Compensation</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-3e2500ca-439a-4d89-a91e-8e5ae3bb990b-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We primarily issue stock-based awards to employees in the form of stock options. We determine compensation expense associated with stock options based on the estimated grant date fair value method using the Black-Scholes valuation model. We recognize compensation expense using a straight-line amortization method over the respective vesting period. </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:ResearchAndDevelopmentExpensePolicy" id="ct-nonNumeric-8e994c85-433e-40db-8051-b3062124bf10" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8e994c85-433e-40db-8051-b3062124bf10-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Research and Development Expenditures</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8e994c85-433e-40db-8051-b3062124bf10-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-8e994c85-433e-40db-8051-b3062124bf10-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-8e994c85-433e-40db-8051-b3062124bf10-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Research and development expenditures are expensed as incurred, and consist primarily of compensation costs, outside services, and expensed materials.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:AdvertisingCostsPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-9a5bc948-f091-45e4-9581-6b983394ab22" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-9a5bc948-f091-45e4-9581-6b983394ab22-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Advertising Expense</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-9a5bc948-f091-45e4-9581-6b983394ab22-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-9a5bc948-f091-45e4-9581-6b983394ab22-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-9a5bc948-f091-45e4-9581-6b983394ab22-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Direct advertising costs are expensed as incurred and consist primarily of E-commerce advertisements, sales enablement, content creation, and other direct costs. Advertising expense was $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-3ebafbb5-6ec7-4089-a45a-9706a39476d8" name="us-gaap:AdvertisingExpense" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">962,049</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> and $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-178bf69e-7983-4e8e-a333-67564af53d5e" name="us-gaap:AdvertisingExpense" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">399,266</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> for years ended December&#160;31, 2021 and 2020, respectively. We also include the cost of attending trade shows under marketing expense. We recorded $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-c627ed50-b72e-4813-80c1-1ee848a45f31" name="us-gaap:MarketingExpense" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">50,267</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> and $</span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"><ix:nonFraction id="ct-nonFraction-979ec3b2-6118-4665-a1b5-6732ee566524" name="us-gaap:MarketingExpense" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2020_To_12_31_2020" unitRef="Unit12" decimals="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal">81,852</ix:nonFraction></span><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> of expense related to those activities for the years ended December&#160;31, 2021 and 2020, respectively.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxPolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-5c7d2320-02b6-44ba-a5f0-bbd5c33defa1" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-5c7d2320-02b6-44ba-a5f0-bbd5c33defa1-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Income Taxes</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-5c7d2320-02b6-44ba-a5f0-bbd5c33defa1-continuation-0" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-5c7d2320-02b6-44ba-a5f0-bbd5c33defa1-continuation-1"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-5c7d2320-02b6-44ba-a5f0-bbd5c33defa1-continuation-1"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">We account for income taxes using the assets and liability method, which recognizes deferred tax assets and liabilities determined based on the difference between the financial statement and tax basis of assets and liabilities using enacted tax rates in effect for the year in which the differences are expected to affect taxable income. Valuation allowances are established to reduce deferred tax assets when, based on available objective evidence, it is more likely than not that the benefit of such assets will not be realized. We recognize in the consolidated financial statements only those tax positions determined to be more likely than not of being sustained.</span></ix:continuation></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><ix:nonNumeric name="us-gaap:EarningsPerSharePolicyTextBlock" id="ct-nonNumeric-9e5cecc1-b197-4a66-b6dd-1fd81a24d9ad" contextRef="Duration_1_1_2021_To_12_31_2021" continuedAt="ct-nonNumeric-9e5cecc1-b197-4a66-b6dd-1fd81a24d9ad-continuation-0"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: bold;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Computation of Net Loss per Common Share</span></ix:nonNumeric></p><ix:continuation id="ct-nonNumeric-9e5cecc1-b197-4a66-b6dd-1fd81a24d9ad-continuation-0"><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">Basic net loss per share is based upon the weighted average number of common shares outstanding. Diluted net loss per share is based on the assumption that all potential common stock equivalents (convertible notes payable, stock options, and warrants) are converted or exercised. The calculation of diluted net loss per share excludes potential common stock equivalents if the effect is anti-dilutive. Our weighted average common shares outstanding for basic and diluted are the same because the effect of the potential common stock equivalents is anti-dilutive.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; The following are a summary of recent accounting developments.</span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;"> </span></p><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;text-align: justify;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;font-style: normal;font-weight: normal;margin: 0;padding: 0;">In August 2020, the FASB issued ASU 2020-06, Accounting for Convertible Instruments and Contracts in an Entity&apos;s Own Equity (&#8220;ASU 2020-06&#8221; or the &#8220;ASU&#8221;). ASU No. 2020-06 requires that the if-converted method of computing diluted Earnings per Share. The company adopted January 1, 2022.</span></p></ix:continuation></div></ix:continuation>

Further losses are anticipated in the development of the Company&#8217;s business raising substantial doubt about the Company&#8217;s ability to continue as a going concern. The ability to continue as a going concern is dependent upon the Company generating profitable operations in the future and/or obtaining the necessary financing to meet its obligations and repay its liabilities arising from normal business operations when they come due. Management intends to finance operating costs over the next twelve months with the proceeds from the sale of securities, and/or revenues from operations. These financial statements do not include any adjustments relating to the recoverability and classification of recorded asset amounts, or amounts and classification of liabilities that might result from this uncertainty.</span></p></ix:continuation><p dir="ltr" style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'serif';font-size: 10pt;line-height: 108%;margin-bottom: .001pt;margin-left: 0;margin-right: 0;margin-top: 0;"><span style="white-space:pre-wrap;
